analysis.core_story_summary: Largest of all the Puranas. Contains eighty eight thousand and one hundred Shlokas (stanzas). Has seven parts- Maheshwar, Vaishnava, Brahma, Kashi, Avanti, Nagar and Prabhasa.

audience_notes.children_8_12: Use simple language, explain divine names as characters in a sacred legend.
audience_notes.teens_13_17: Connect events to decision-making, identity, and consequences.
audience_notes.young_adults_18_30: Highlight ethics, leadership, and social relevance.
audience_notes.devotional_traditional: Preserve sacred framing and theological terms respectfully.
audience_notes.global_general: Keep cultural context brief and avoid insider assumptions.

sensitivity_notes: Contains conflict imagery that may need age-appropriate framing.
Includes intense mythic elements (curses, demons, destruction).

redesigned_story_by_demographic.children_8_12: **SKANDA PURANA — Kids (8-12):** Largest of all the Puranas. Contains eighty eight thousand and one hundred Shlokas (stanzas). Has seven parts- Maheshwar, Vaishnava, Brahma, Kashi, Avanti, Nagar and Prabhasa. This story teaches the cost of conflict. Think of it like a legend that helps us choose kindness, courage, and respect.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.teens_13_17: **SKANDA PURANA — Teens (13-17):** Largest of all the Puranas. Contains eighty eight thousand and one hundred Shlokas (stanzas). Has seven parts- Maheshwar, Vaishnava, Brahma, Kashi, Avanti, Nagar and Prabhasa. For teens, the key idea is the cost of conflict. It shows how choices, discipline, and character shape outcomes.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.young_adults_18_30: **SKANDA PURANA — Young Adults:** Largest of all the Puranas. Contains eighty eight thousand and one hundred Shlokas (stanzas). Has seven parts- Maheshwar, Vaishnava, Brahma, Kashi, Avanti, Nagar and Prabhasa. A modern takeaway is the cost of conflict. The narrative can be read as a reflection on purpose, responsibility, and values-driven action.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.devotional_traditional: **SKANDA PURANA — Devotional/Traditional Readers:** Largest of all the Puranas. Contains eighty eight thousand and one hundred Shlokas (stanzas). Has seven parts- Maheshwar, Vaishnava, Brahma, Kashi, Avanti, Nagar and Prabhasa. The episode highlights the cost of conflict. It preserves reverence for divine agency while encouraging faithful practice and humility.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.global_general: **SKANDA PURANA — Global General Audience:** Largest of all the Puranas. Contains eighty eight thousand and one hundred Shlokas (stanzas). Has seven parts- Maheshwar, Vaishnava, Brahma, Kashi, Avanti, Nagar and Prabhasa. In universal terms, it explores the cost of conflict. Cultural names are preserved while the message is presented in accessible contemporary English.
